items.title: ⚠️ The Swiss franc will stay aloft against the yen as the Bank of Japan falls behind the curve…
items.content:
⚠️ The Swiss franc will stay aloft against the yen as the Bank of Japan falls behind the curve in its fight against inflation.

CHF/JPY has surged past 200, in line with a case made here, essentially putting the cross into uncharted territory after an untiring rally that is now in its eighth year. The pace of gains may slow, as the Swiss National Bank is likely watching with a hawk’s eye given its long-stated wariness toward excessive currency strength.
However, the direction of travel is decidedly north for the cross. While the surge in energy prices will affect both economies, there is a crucial difference: unlike Japan, the majority of Switzerland’s crude imports are sourced from outside the Middle East.

The starting point matters, too: Switzerland had too little by way of inflation heading into the Iran war, unlike Japan, where the BOJ was already behind in taming price pressures. Now that energy prices have spiraled, Japan faces a higher import bill and those costs will cascade down the economy to worsen the nation’s inflation profile. With the BOJ still reluctant to step up its pace of policy tightening, the yen will be exposed in general against most currencies, as David Savage pointed out. That will be particularly so against the franc.
瑞郎兌日圓將持續走強，因日本央行在對抗通膨方面落後於形勢。CHF/JPY 已突破 200 關口，與此前在此提出的觀點一致，這場歷經八年、未曾停歇的漲勢，基本上已將該交叉匯率推入前所未見的領域。
漲勢可能趨緩，因為 Swiss National Bank 鑑於其長期以來對過度貨幣走強的警惕，很可能正密切關注。然而，該交叉匯率的走勢明確向上。儘管能源價格飆升將影響兩國經濟，但存在一個關鍵差異：與日本不同，瑞士大部分的原油進口並非來自中東地區。
起點也很重要：與日本不同，瑞士在伊朗戰爭爆發前的通膨水準過低，而日本則是 BOJ（日本央行）在抑制物價壓力方面早已落後。如今能源價格飆漲，日本面臨更高的進口成本，這些費用將在經濟體系中層層傳導，進一步惡化該國的通膨情勢。
日本央行（BOJ）仍不願加快其政策緊縮步伐，日圓兌大多數貨幣將普遍面臨貶值壓力，正如 David Savage 所指出的。兌瑞郎（franc）的情況尤其如此。 @parisprogram

items.title: Markets began the week focused on the risk of prolonged energy price increases before being whi…
items.content:
Markets began the week focused on the risk of prolonged energy price increases before being whipsawed by President Trump’s suggestion the conflict could end soon. Stocks are rallying today but little has changed fundamentally, with questions over the Strait’s reopening still unresolved. What has risen is macro uncertainty, lifting volatility that will weigh on global equities.

The impact of higher volatility works through three main channels. First, greater uncertainty raises the equity risk premium. As future cash flows become less predictable, investors demand higher compensation for risk, leading to valuation multiple compression, particularly in longer-duration growth assets. At the same time, larger price swings reduce risk-adjusted returns, making equity exposure less attractive.
Second, volatility can trigger mechanical de-risking. For example, risk parity strategies use volatility targeting, so when vol rises the amount invested in equities declines automatically. Higher hedging costs and margin requirements reinforce this adjustment.

Third, volatility tightens financial conditions. Wider credit spreads and a rising equity risk premium increase the cost of capital, curb issuance and buybacks, and weigh on confidence. In that sense, volatility acts as a form of passive tightening.

With no resolution to the conflict in Iran firmly in sight, volatility is likely to remain elevated. That persistence suggests another ongoing headwind for global equity markets.
市場本週開盤時聚焦於能源價格長期上漲的風險，隨後因 Trump 總統暗示衝突可能很快結束而劇烈波動。股市今日反彈，但基本面幾乎沒有改變，海峽重新開放的問題仍未解決。真正上升的是總體不確定性，推升波動性並將對全球股市造成壓力。波動性升高的影響主要透過三個管道發揮作用。
首先，不確定性升高會推升股票風險溢價。隨著未來現金流變得更難預測，投資人要求更高的風險補償，導致估值倍數壓縮，尤其是存續期較長的成長型資產。同時，較大的價格波動會降低經風險調整後的報酬，使得股票曝險的吸引力下降。其次，波動性可能觸發機械性的去風險操作。
舉例來說，風險平價策略採用波動率目標設定，因此當波動率上升時，投資於股票的資金會自動減少。較高的避險成本與保證金要求強化了這項調整。第三，波動率會緊縮金融環境。信用利差擴大與股票風險溢價上升會提高資金成本、抑制發行與庫藏股回購，並打擊市場信心。
從這個角度來看，波動性形同於一種被動的緊縮。由於伊朗衝突尚無明確的解決方案，波動性可能持續居高不下。這種持續性意味著全球股市將面臨另一股持續的不利因素。 @parisprogram

items.title: Global equity futures are continuing to pick up in early European trading, with the Eurostoxx 5…
items.content:
Global equity futures are continuing to pick up in early European trading, with the Eurostoxx 50 contract now up 2.2% and the S&P adding 0.2%. That’s come alongside another leg lower in crude and natural gas prices. Meanwhile, European bonds are rising, led by the periphery.

The same dynamics are being reflected in UK central bank pricing. BOE rate bets, which showed a near 90% chance of a hike this year at one point yesterday, now show a 50% chance of a cut.

As mentioned yesterday, the case for the BOE to tighten policy was already hampered by the weakness in the labour market compared to the 2022 Ukraine conflict and financial conditions that had began tightening. Indeed, there is already a behavioural element on display with UK consumers. Data from Barclays from a survey carried out after the conflict began showed that nearly half were “already taking action to adjust, for example by cutting energy use, spending less on discretionary items or saving more”.

As Ven noted though, the recovery isn’t absolute. German two-year yields have shed 6bps but still remain roughly 25bps above pre-conflict levels.

Gilts yields are set to open lower too, potentially even more so than German counterparts given the extent of yesterday’s moves. However, context does remain key in interpreting these moves.
全球股市期貨在歐洲早盤交易中持續上揚，Eurostoxx 50 期貨合約目前上漲 2.2%，S&P 則增加 0.2%。這與原油和天然氣價格再度下跌同步發生。同時，歐洲債券正在上漲，由邊緣國家領漲。同樣的動態也反映在英國央行定價中。
英國央行（BOE）的升息押注，昨日一度顯示今年升息機率接近 90%，如今則顯示降息機率為 50%。如昨日所述，相較於 2022 年烏克蘭衝突時期，英國勞動市場疲弱，加上金融環境已開始緊縮，這些因素早已削弱英國央行緊縮政策的理由。事實上，英國消費者行為已經出現變化。
根據 Barclays 在衝突爆發後進行的調查數據顯示，近半數受訪者「已經採取行動進行調整，例如減少能源使用、削減非必要支出或增加儲蓄」。然而正如 Ven 所指出的，復甦並非全面性的。德國兩年期公債殖利率已下跌 6 基點，但仍較衝突前水準高出約 25 基點。
英國公債殖利率預計也將開低，考量昨日漲幅之劇烈，跌幅甚至可能較德國公債更為顯著。然而，解讀這些走勢時，背景脈絡仍是關鍵。 @parisprogram

items.title: Barring a new twist from headlines, bonds in the UK and the euro area are on course for a relie…
items.content:
Barring a new twist from headlines, bonds in the UK and the euro area are on course for a relief rally at the open. It won’t be back to business as usual though.

While President Donald Trump calmed the markets with his statement that the Iran war could be over soon, there is more to ending the conflict than just resorting to soothing words. Tehran has been silent, the Strait of Hormuz is still jammed, and Brent crude prices are still hovering around $95 a barrel. Meanwhile European natural-gas prices are still up a staggering 75% since the war began.

Even if the US were to conclude its most intensive phase of aerial operations in a few weeks, oil and natural-gas prices may not go back to where they were before the conflict began, so the inflationary impact of even a short war — which is the markets’ most optimistic scenario — can’t be wished away.

As I noted yesterday, both the UK and the euro-area economies are vulnerable to the energy shock given their reliance on imported oil. Even though the UK is less reliant than the euro zone on imports, the impact of the surge in energy prices will be greater in the UK given that inflation wasn’t already under control before the war.

The better mood today may see pricing for central bank tightening mellow, and support bonds in the process, but it is too early to rule out the prospect of hikes entirely. That risks foiling any long-term recovery in bonds beyond today’s initial kneejerk moves.
除非頭條出現新的轉折，英國與歐元區債券可望在開盤時迎來一波反彈行情。然而，這並不代表將回歸常態。儘管美國總統 Donald Trump 表示伊朗戰爭可能很快結束，暫時穩定了市場情緒，但要真正終結這場衝突，光靠安撫性言論顯然仍嫌不足。
德黑蘭方面始終保持沉默，荷莫茲海峽（Strait of Hormuz）依舊壅塞，而布蘭特原油（Brent crude）價格仍徘徊在每桶 95 美元左右。同時，歐洲天然氣價格自戰爭爆發以來仍大幅上漲 75%，漲幅驚人。
即使美國在未來幾週內結束最密集的空襲行動，原油與天然氣價格也可能無法回到衝突爆發前的水準，因此即便是短暫戰爭所帶來的通膨衝擊——這也是市場最樂觀的情境——也無法輕忽。正如我昨日所言，英國與歐元區經濟均因高度依賴進口石油而容易受到能源衝擊的影響。
儘管英國對進口的依賴程度低於歐元區，但由於通膨在戰前尚未獲得控制，能源價格飆漲對英國的衝擊將更為嚴重。今日市場情緒好轉可能使央行緊縮政策的定價趨於緩和，並在此過程中支撐債券，但現在完全排除升息可能性仍為時過早。
這恐將阻礙債券市場在今日初步的膝反射式波動之後，出現任何長期復甦。 @parisprogram

items.title: Futures are pointing to a higher open for European stocks, tracking gains in Asia and on Wall S…
items.content:
Futures are pointing to a higher open for European stocks, tracking gains in Asia and on Wall Street after President Donald Trump signaled the Iran war may be nearing an end. Brent crude futures are hovering around $93 a barrel, down about 5% from Monday’s settlement but higher versus the close. US equity contracts are only a tad lower, but still signaling this bounce in risk has its limitations.

Trump did temper expectations slightly as he later said he didn’t believe the conflict would be over this week. And several Middle Eastern countries have since announced missile threats, sounded sirens or intercepted drones -- discussions around resuming traffic through the Strait of Hormuz will be likely remain on hold until that sort of activity subsides, limiting the downside in energy prices and keeping broader risk sentiment in check.

Treasuries fall, pushing US 10-year yields up 2 bps to 4.11%. European government bond futures are higher as they have some catching up to do with Monday’s late-day moves. The Bloomberg Dollar Spot Index drops 0.2%. The kiwi lags slightly, weakening 0.1% against the greenback. Precious metals rise, as does Bitcoin.

Five Things You Need to Know to Start Your Day: Europe
歐洲股市期貨指向高開，追隨亞洲及華爾街漲勢，此前美國總統 Donald Trump 暗示伊朗戰爭可能即將結束。Brent 原油期貨徘徊在每桶 $93 附近，較週一結算價下跌約 5%，但高於收盤價。美國股票期貨僅小幅走低，但仍顯示這波風險資產反彈有其侷限。
川普稍後確實稍微降低了市場預期，表示他認為衝突本週內不會結束。而隨後多個中東國家宣布飛彈威脅、響起防空警報或攔截無人機——關於恢復荷莫茲海峽（Strait of Hormuz）航運的討論很可能將持續擱置，直到這類活動平息為止，這將限制能源價格的下跌空間，並使整體風險情緒受到抑制。
美國公債下跌，推升美國 10 年期公債殖利率上揚 2 基點至 4.11%。歐洲政府債券期貨走高，因需追趕週一尾盤的漲勢。Bloomberg Dollar Spot Index 下跌 0.2%。紐幣表現相對疲弱，對美元貶值 0.1%。貴金屬與 Bitcoin 同步上漲。歐洲市場開盤前必知的五件事 @parisprogram

items.title: AUD/USD is nudging higher into European session on a stream of hawkish comments from the RBA’s…
items.content:
AUD/USD is nudging higher into European session on a stream of hawkish comments from the RBA’s Hauser, which suggests he at least favors an interest rate hike next week.

Hauser says inflation is toxic and needs to be controlled. Let’s see if the rest of the central bankers agree and are willing to hike amid a volatile energy price environment.
AUD/USD 在歐洲時段盤初小幅走高，因澳洲央行（RBA）Hauser 接連發表鷹派言論，顯示他至少傾向於下周升息。

Hauser 表示通膨具有毒性，必須加以控制。讓我們觀察其他央行官員是否認同這一觀點，並願意在能源價格波動的環境中升息。 @parisprogram

items.title: 💥 Bonds will be the biggest laggard among US assets as traders brace for the prospect of an inf…
items.content:
💥 Bonds will be the biggest laggard among US assets as traders brace for the prospect of an inflation shock from prolonged conflict in the Middle East, the latest Markets Pulse survey finds. Meanwhile, South Korean stocks face substantial risks from the geopolitical event.

After gyrations in oil roiled global assets, WTI crude is now trading below $90 a barrel, which is still substantially above pre-Iran war levels below $70. While stocks in Asia are mostly in the green after a positive close on Wall Street on Monday, the outlook remains murky as Garfield Reynolds argues given the lingering impact from energy and trade shocks.
The rise in crude prices would materially drive US inflation higher if the conflict lasts a month or more, said nearly three-quarters of the 402 respondents to the poll conducted March 3-6. That said, US equities will be resilient, with 61% saying they don’t expect the Iran war to trigger a 10% correction this month, the survey finds.

For more on the survey’s findings, read here.
💥 最新 Markets Pulse 調查發現，隨著交易員為中東長期衝突可能引發的通膨衝擊做好準備，債券將成為美國資產中表現最為落後的類別。同時，南韓股市面臨來自地緣政治事件的重大風險。在油價劇烈波動擾動全球資產後，WTI 原油目前交易價格已低於每桶 90 美元，但仍大幅高於伊朗戰爭前低於 70 美元的水準。
亞洲股市大多上漲，追隨華爾街週一的正面收盤表現，但展望依然黯淡，正如 Garfield Reynolds 所指出，能源與貿易衝擊的持續影響仍未消退。這項於 3 月 3 日至 6 日進行的調查顯示，402 位受訪者中近四分之三表示，若衝突持續一個月或更久，原油價格上漲將大幅推升美國通膨。
話雖如此，美國股市將展現韌性，61% 的受訪者表示他們預期伊朗戰爭不會在本月引發 10% 的回檔，調查顯示。關於調查結果的更多內容，請見此處。 @parisprogram

items.title: Crude prices and the yen are trading roughly in line with Friday’s close, even after Monday’s w…
items.content:
Crude prices and the yen are trading roughly in line with Friday’s close, even after Monday’s wild swings. USD/JPY is trading below 158, a level that had been viewed as a ceiling following January’s rate check, but now may be seen as less constraining with the inflation outlook deteriorating.

Higher energy prices add near‑term inflation pressure while worsening affordability, dampening growth and making policy tightening increasingly problematic. As George Lei noted, options continue to signal limited intervention risk.

Meanwhile, rate hike expectations for 2026 have moderated alongside yen weakness, with the odds of an April increase down to about 60% from close to 80% about a month ago. If OIS traders continue to pare rate hike bets for 2026, concerns about quickening inflation are likely to weigh more heavily on the yen, generating potential for USD/JPY to test cycle highs above 161.
原油價格與日圓大致維持在週五收盤價位附近交易，即便週一曾出現劇烈波動。USD/JPY 現報 158 下方，該水準在 1 月日本央行進行利率檢查後曾被視為天花板，但隨著通膨展望惡化，現在看來可能已不具那麼強的約束力。能源價格上漲在短期內增添通膨壓力，同時惡化負擔能力、抑制成長，並使政策緊縮日益棘手。
正如 George Lei 所指出的，選擇權持續顯示干預風險有限。與此同時，隨著日圓走軟，市場對 2026 年升息的預期已有所降溫，4 月升息的機率從約一個月前的近 80% 降至約 60%。若 OIS 交易員持續削減對 2026 年的升息押注，對通膨加速的擔憂可能對日圓造成更大壓力，使 USD/JPY 有機會測試 161 上方的週期高點。 @parisprogram

items.title: 💥 Brent crude is creeping higher in the Asian session amid warnings of missile threats across t…
items.content:
💥 Brent crude is creeping higher in the Asian session amid warnings of missile threats across the Middle East, which means traders will be quick to reload long exposure.

Macro investors aren’t going to let themselves be caught flat footed again by a sudden energy price spike. Which will leave a war premium embedded into oil for a while yet, even if hostilities are reduced.

After the wide swings seen on Monday, Brent futures are still well above the breakout zone established at the beginning of March. That’s another encouragement for traders to maintain a bullish disposition toward crude.
💥 布蘭特原油在亞洲時段緩步走高，因中東各地傳出飛彈威脅警報，這意味著交易員將迅速重新建立多頭部位。總體投資者不會再讓自己因能源價格突然飆漲而措手不及。即使敵對行動降溫，戰爭溢價仍將在一段時間內持續嵌入油價之中。
週一經歷大幅波動後，Brent 期貨價格仍遠高於 3 月初確立的突破區間。這對於交易員維持對原油的看多態度，又是一項鼓舞。 @parisprogram

items.content:
原油從川普總統燃起美伊衝突迅速落幕的希望後所觸及的低點緩步回升。這導致亞洲股市動能減弱，美國指數期貨跌幅擴大。

正如我先前所論，投資人需警惕油價衝擊所釋放的潛在長期影響。WTI 與 Brent 原油雙雙回升至每桶 90 美元以上，正凸顯了這項疑慮。
